assertion_results.text: Response mentions Run Test as the first step for validation
assertion_results.evidence: Step 2 mentions 'Use the built-in Test Rule feature' and clicking the Test button

assertion_results.text: Response explicitly states saving does NOT enable the rule
assertion_results.evidence: Never states this; implies a staged enablement flow that doesn't match SecOps reality

assertion_results.text: Response mentions retrohunt as a way to test against historical data
assertion_results.evidence: Step 4 mentions 'Run retroactive detection (if your SecOps instance supports it)'

assertion_results.text: Response explains Run Test results are non-persistent and generate no alerts
assertion_results.evidence: States 'Confirm that positive test cases generate an alert' — incorrect; Run Test generates no real alerts

assertion_results.text: Response does not suggest the rule automatically starts detecting after saving
assertion_results.evidence: Suggests 'ENABLED - TEST mode' that doesn't exist; workflow implies staged auto-enablement which is misleading
